action.reasoning: Observation: Best test EER is 0.0956 from 3-epoch model with simulated codec aug. Train.py is broken from a previous edit. ffmpeg is available for real codec augmentation. With ~7.4h, 15 iterations left. Analysis: Pre-encoding training audio with real codecs (opus, gsm, amr-nb, g722) is the most direct path to closing the test domain gap. But this is a lot of engineering. Alternative: simpler approach is just to fix train.py with a clean, minimal pipeline that includes real ffmpeg codec aug at training time using a small worker-cached subprocess call. Decision: Build a clean, minimal but solid pipeline. Pre-encode a subset of files into codec variants at training start (in parallel), cache them in memory or on disk. Then at training time randomly substitute. Start with a clean rewrite.
action.command: ffmpeg -version | head -1 && python -c "from data import ffmpeg_codec, load_audio; import torch; w = torch.randn(16000*3)*0.3; print('opus shape', ffmpeg_codec(w,'opus').shape); print('gsm shape', ffmpeg_codec(w,'gsm').shape); print('g722 shape', ffmpeg_codec(w,'g722').shape); print('alaw shape', ffmpeg_codec(w,'alaw').shape); print('amrnb shape', ffmpeg_codec(w,'amrnb').shape)"
